title: Hot Rolling
author: JSL EKOS
description: This module explains the Hot Rolling process in stainless steel manufacturing, where continuously cast slabs are reheated and rolled into coils or plates with the required thickness and dimensions.
learningObjectives: Understand the purpose of Hot Rolling.
Learn the Hot Rolling process flow.
Identify major equipment used in the Hot Rolling Mill.

content.introduction: Hot Rolling is the first rolling process after continuous casting. Steel slabs are reheated and passed through a series of rolling stands where their thickness is gradually reduced while maintaining the required mechanical properties.
content.purpose: The purpose of Hot Rolling is to reduce slab thickness, improve workability, prepare material for downstream processing, and produce hot rolled coils or plates.
content.workingPrinciple: Steel slabs are heated to rolling temperature inside a reheating furnace. The heated slab passes through multiple rolling stands where compressive forces reduce its thickness before coiling.
processSteps: Transfer hot rolled coil for further processing.
safetyPrecautions: Maintain safe distance from hot slabs.
Observe crane and coil handling safety procedures.
content.summary: Hot Rolling transforms cast slabs into hot rolled coils through controlled heating and rolling operations. Proper process control ensures consistent dimensions, good surface quality, and prepares the material for further processing.
